title: Phase-switchable nonreciprocal entanglement via magnon squeezing in ring-cavity optomagnomechanics
abstract: Cavity optomagnomechanics provides a versatile platform to explore macroscopic quantum correlations, particularly nonreciprocal entanglement. In this work, we propose a theoretical scheme to generate switchable bipartite and tripartite entanglement in an optomagnomechanical ring cavity by exploiting phase-controlled magnon squeezing. Indeed, two spatially separated ferrimagnetic YIG microbridges become entangled through their magnetostriction-mediated coupling to mechanical motion and a common cavity field via radiation-pressure interaction. The squeezing process introduces two phase-dependent contributions to the magnon response, namely an effective detuning shift $Δ_{θ_j}$ and a quadrature-damping contribution $κ_{θ_j}$, both of which reverse sign upon a $π$ phase shift, providing an in situ control to switch the entanglement response. The nonreciprocal entanglement is defined operationally through the asymmetric entanglement response under the phase reversal $θ_j \to θ_j + π$, quantified by normalized contrast ratios $C_E$ and $C_{\mathcal{R}}$, which measure the relative difference between the entanglement obtained at $θ_j$ and at the phase-reversed configuration $θ_j+π$. The resulting phase-tuning method provides a flexible and robust route to achieve high-contrast bipartite and tripartite entanglement within stable parameter regions, establishing magnon squeezing as a practical quantum resource for switchable quantum correlations in hybrid platforms.
comment: 7 figures. Accepted for publication in Phys. Rev. A

bite.hook: A new ring-cavity setup uses magnon squeezing to switch quantum entanglement on and off with a simple phase shift.
tldr: The authors proposed a theoretical scheme to generate switchable bipartite and tripartite entanglement in an optomagnomechanical ring cavity.
They modeled two YIG microbridges coupled to mechanical motion and a cavity field, using phase-controlled magnon squeezing to introduce phase-dependent responses.
They found that a simple pi phase shift reverses the sign of the magnon response, enabling high-contrast, nonreciprocal control of quantum entanglement.
bite.whyItMatters: This provides a highly flexible and robust way to control quantum entanglement in hybrid systems, which is a crucial step toward building scalable quantum networks and processors.
bite.eli5: Imagine two tiny magnets connected by light and sound waves. By twisting the phase of the light like a dial, we can instantly turn their quantum connection (entanglement) on or off, or even make it one-way.

glossary.shortDef: The study of interactions between light, mechanical vibrations, and magnetic excitations (magnons).
glossary.eli5Def: A field of physics where scientists use light, tiny vibrations, and magnetic waves to talk to each other inside a small chip.

glossary.shortDef: A quantum state preparation that reduces uncertainty in one magnetic property at the expense of another.
glossary.eli5Def: Squeezing a balloon so it gets very thin in one direction but longer in another, making one property of a magnet extremely precise.

glossary.shortDef: A system behavior that is asymmetric when the direction of propagation or excitation is reversed.
glossary.eli5Def: A one-way street for physics, where signals can travel from A to B, but not from B to A.
